   159→
   160→@dataclass
   161→class Proposal:
   162→    """A proposal to merge a draft version."""
   163→    proposal_id: int
   164→    name: str
   165→    scope: Scope
   166→    draft_version: int
   167→    base_version: int
   168→    author: str
   169→    title: str | None
   170→    description: str | None
   171→    labels: list[str]
   172→    quorum: ApprovalPolicy  # Snapshot of policy at creation time
   173→    status: str
   174→    tally: Tally
   175→    diffs: DiffArtifacts
   176→    created_at: float  # timestamp
   177→
   178→    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   179→        return {
   180→            'proposal_id': self.proposal_id,
   181→            'name': self.name,
   182→            'scope': self.scope,
   183→            'draft_version': self.draft_version,
   184→            'base_version': self.base_version,
   185→            'author': self.author,
   186→            'title': self.title,
   187→            'description': self.description,
   188→            'labels': sorted(self.labels),
   189→            'quorum': self.quorum.to_dict(),
   190→            'status': self.status,
   191→            'tally': self.tally.to_dict(),
   192→            'diffs': self.diffs.to_dict(),
   193→        }
   194→
   195→
   196→class ReviewRecord:
   197→    """Stores a review decision."""
   198→    def __init__(self, actor: str, decision: str, message: str | None):
   199→        self.actor = actor
   200→        self.decision = decision  # 'approve' or 'reject'
   201→        self.message = message
   202→
   203→
   204→class ProposalStorage:
   205→    """In-memory storage for proposals."""
   206→
   207→    def __init__(self):
   208→        # proposal_id -> Proposal
   209→        self._proposals: dict[int, Proposal] = {}
   210→        # Counter for generating proposal IDs
   211→        self._next_proposal_id = 1
   212→        # Reviews per proposal
   213→        self._reviews: dict[int, dict[str, ReviewRecord]] = {}  # proposal_id -> actor -> ReviewRecord
   214→
   215→    def create_proposal(self, proposal: Proposal) -> Proposal:
   216→        """Create a new proposal."""
   217→        proposal.proposal_id = self._next_proposal_id
   218→        self._next_proposal_id += 1
   219→        self._proposals[proposal.proposal_id] = proposal
   220→        self._reviews[proposal.proposal_id] = {}
   221→        return proposal
   222→
   223→    def get_proposal(self, proposal_id: int) -> Proposal:
   224→        """Get a proposal by ID."""
   225→        if proposal_id not in self._proposals:
   226→            raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Proposal {proposal_id} not found')
   227→        return self._proposals[proposal_id]
   228→
   229→    def update_proposal(self, proposal_id: int, proposal: Proposal) -> None:
   230→        """Update a proposal."""
   231→        self._proposals[proposal_id] = proposal
   232→
   233→    def list_proposals(self, name: str, scope: Scope,
   234→                       status_filter: str | None) -> list[Proposal]:
   235→        """List proposals for a (name, scope) pair, optionally filtered by status."""
   236→        proposals = [p for p in self._proposals.values()
   237→                     if p.name == name and p.scope == scope]
   238→
   239→        if status_filter is not None and status_filter != 'any':
   240→            proposals = [p for p in proposals if p.status == status_filter]
   241→
   242→        return sorted(proposals, key=lambda p: p.proposal_id)
   243→
   244→    def add_review(self, proposal_id: int, review: ReviewRecord) -> None:
   245→        """Add or update a review for a proposal."""
   246→        if proposal_id not in self._reviews:
   247→            raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Proposal {proposal_id} not found')
   248→
   249→        reviews = self._reviews[proposal_id]
   250→        if len(reviews) >= MAX_REVIEWS_PER_PROPOSAL:
   251→            raise ConfigError('conflict', f'Maximum {MAX_REVIEWS_PER_PROPOSAL} reviews per proposal')
   252→
   253→        reviews[review.actor] = review
   254→
   255→    def get_reviews(self, proposal_id: int) -> dict[str, ReviewRecord]:
   256→        """Get all reviews for a proposal."""
   257→        if proposal_id not in self._reviews:
   258→            raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Proposal {proposal_id} not found')
   259→        return self._reviews[proposal_id]
   260→
   261→
   262→# =============================================================================
   263→# Diff Generation
   264→# =============================================================================
   265→
   266→def compute_drafts_storage_diff(base_config: dict[str, Any],
   267→                                 draft_config: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   268→    """Compute RFC 6902 patch between two stored configs."""
   269→    patch = []
   270→
   271→    # Find removed and modified keys
   272→    base_keys = set(base_config.keys())
   273→    draft_keys = set(draft_config.keys())
   274→
   275→    # Removed keys
   276→    for key in sorted(base_keys - draft_keys):
   277→        patch.append({'op': 'remove', 'path': f'/{key}'})
   278→
   279→    # Modified or added keys
   280→    for key in sorted(draft_keys):
   281→        if key in base_config:
   282→            if base_config[key] != draft_config[key]:
   283→                patch.append({'op': 'replace', 'path': f'/{key}', 'value': draft_config[key]})
   284→        else:
   285→            patch.append({'op': 'add', 'path': f'/{key}', 'value': draft_config[key]})
   286→
   287→    return patch
   288→
   289→
   290→def compute_drafts_resolved_diff(base_resolved: dict[str, Any],
   291→                                  draft_resolved: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   292→    """Compute RFC 6902 patch between two resolved configs."""
   293→    # For nested objects, we need a more sophisticated approach
   294→    def deep_diff(base, draft, path=''):
   295→        diff = []
   296→
   297→        if isinstance(base, dict) and isinstance(draft, dict):
   298→            # Find removed keys
   299→            for key in sorted(set(base.keys()) - set(draft.keys())):
   300→                diff.append({'op': 'remove', 'path': f'{path}/{key}' if path else f'/{key}'})
   301→
   302→            # Find added/modified keys
   303→            for key in sorted(set(draft.keys()) - set(base.keys())):
   304→                diff.append({'op': 'add', 'path': f'{path}/{key}' if path else f'/{key}', 'value': draft[key]})
   305→
   306→            # Find modified values
   307→            for key in sorted(set(base.keys()) & set(draft.keys())):
   308→                if base[key] != draft[key]:
   309→                    diff.extend(deep_diff(base[key], draft[key], f'{path}/{key}' if path else f'/{key}'))
   310→        elif isinstance(base, list) and isinstance(draft, list):
   311→            # For lists, treat as replacement
   312→            if base != draft:
   313→                diff.append({'op': 'replace', 'path': path, 'value': draft})
   314→        elif base != draft:
   315→            # Primitive value changed
   316→            diff.append({'op': 'replace', 'path': path, 'value': draft})
   317→
   318→        return diff
   319→
   320→    patch = deep_diff(base_resolved, draft_resolved)
   321→
   322→    # Sort lexicographically by path, then by operation type (remove, replace, add)
   323→    op_order = {'remove': 0, 'replace': 1, 'add': 2}
   324→    patch.sort(key=lambda x: (x['path'], op_order.get(x['op'], 3)))
   325→
   326→    return patch
   327→
   328→
   329→def compute_includes_changes(base_includes: tuple[IncludeRef, ...],
   330→                              draft_includes: tuple[IncludeRef, ...]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   331→    """Compute changes to the includes list."""
   332→    changes = []
   333→
   334→    # Convert to dict for easier comparison
   335→    base_dict = {i: ref for i, ref in enumerate(base_includes)}
   336→    draft_dict = {i: ref for i, ref in enumerate(draft_includes)}
   337→
   338→    # Find removed includes
   339→    for idx in sorted(set(base_dict.keys()) - set(draft_dict.keys())):
   340→        changes.append({
   341→            'op': 'remove',
   342→            'index': idx,
   343→            'ref': base_dict[idx].to_dict()
   344→        })
   345→
   346→    # Find added includes
   347→    for idx in sorted(set(draft_dict.keys()) - set(base_dict.keys())):
   348→        changes.append({
   349→            'op': 'add',
   350→            'index': idx,
   351→            'ref': draft_dict[idx].to_dict()
   352→        })
   353→
   354→    # Find updated includes (version pin changes)
   355→    for idx in sorted(set(base_dict.keys()) & set(draft_dict.keys())):
   356→        base_ref = base_dict[idx]
   357→        draft_ref = draft_dict[idx]
   358→        if base_ref.version != draft_ref.version:
   359→            changes.append({
   360→                'op': 'update',
   361→                'index': idx,
   362→                'from_version': base_ref.version,
   363→                'to_version': draft_ref.version,
   364→                'ref': draft_ref.to_dict()
   365→            })
   366→
   367→    return changes
   368→
   369→
   370→def format_human_diff(diffs: DiffArtifacts,
   371→                       base_config: dict[str, Any],
   372→                       draft_config: dict[str, Any],
   373→                       base_includes: tuple[IncludeRef, ...],
   374→                       draft_includes: tuple[IncludeRef, ...]) -> list[str]:
   375→    """Generate human-readable diff lines."""
   376→    human = []
   377→
   378→    # Process raw JSON patch for human diff
   379→    for op in diffs.raw_json_patch:
   380→        op_type = op['op']
   381→        path = op['path']
   382→
   383→        if op_type == 'remove':
   384→            human.append(f'DELETE {path}')
   385→        elif op_type == 'replace':
   386→            old_val = get_nested_value(base_config, path)
   387→            new_val = op.get('value')
   388→            human.append(f'REPLACE {path}: {to_canonical_json(old_val).strip()} -> {to_canonical_json(new_val).strip()}')
   389→        elif op_type == 'add':
   390→            value = op.get('value')
   391→            human.append(f'SET {path}: {to_canonical_json(value).strip()}')
   392→
   393→    # Process includes changes
   394→    for change in diffs.includes_changes:
   395→        op = change['op']
   396→        idx = change['index']
   397→        ref = change.get('ref', {})
   398→        name = ref.get('name', '?')
   399→        scope = ref.get('scope', {})
   400→        scope_str = ','.join(f'{k}={v}' for k, v in sorted(scope.items()))
   401→        version = ref.get('version')
   402→
   403→        scope_json = to_canonical_json(scope).strip()
   404→        version_str = str(version) if version is not None else 'null'
   405→
   406→        if op == 'add':
   407→            human.append(f'INCLUDE_ADD [{idx}] {name}@{scope_json} v={version_str}')
   408→        elif op == 'remove':
   409→            human.append(f'INCLUDE_REMOVE [{idx}] {name}@{scope_json} v={version_str}')
   410→        elif op == 'update':
   411→            from_v = change.get('from_version')
   412→            to_v = change.get('to_version')
   413→            human.append(f'INCLUDE_UPDATE [{idx}] name@{scope_json}: {from_v} -> {to_v}')
   414→
   415→    # Sort: DELETE, REPLACE, SET, then INCLUDE_* alphabetically
   416→    def sort_key(s):
   417→        if s.startswith('DELETE'):
   418→            return (0, s)
   419→        elif s.startswith('REPLACE'):
   420→            return (1, s)
   421→        elif s.startswith('SET'):
   422→            return (2, s)
   423→        elif s.startswith('INCLUDE_'):
   424→            return (3, s)
   425→        return (4, s)
   426→
   427→    human.sort(key=sort_key)
   428→    return human
   429→
   430→
   431→def get_nested_value(config: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:
   432→    """Get a value from a nested dict using a JSON Pointer path."""
   433→    if path == '/':
   434→        return config
   435→
   436→    parts = path.strip('/').split('/')
   437→    current = config
   438→
   439→    for part in parts:
   440→        # Decode URL-encoded characters
   441→        part = part.replace('~1', '/').replace('~0', '~')
   442→        if isinstance(current, dict) and part in current:
   443→            current = current[part]
   444→        else:
   445→            return None
   446→
   447→    return current
   448→
   449→
   450→def compute_diff_artifacts(base_version: ConfigVersion,
   451→                            draft_version: ConfigVersion,
   452→                            storage: ConfigStorage) -> DiffArtifacts:
   453→    """Compute all diff artifacts between base and draft versions."""
   454→
   455→    # Compute raw JSON patch
   456→    raw_patch = compute_drafts_storage_diff(base_version.config, draft_version.config)
   457→
   458→    # Resolve both configs for comparison
   459→    base_resolved, _ = resolve_config(storage, base_version.name,
   460→                                       base_version.scope, base_version.version)
   461→    draft_resolved, _ = resolve_config(storage, draft_version.name,
   462→                                        draft_version.scope, draft_version.version)
   463→
   464→    # Compute resolved JSON patch
   465→    resolved_patch = compute_drafts_resolved_diff(base_resolved, draft_resolved)
   466→
   467→    # Compute includes changes
   468→    includes_changes = compute_includes_changes(base_version.includes, draft_version.includes)
   469→
   470→    # Create temporary diffs object for human formatting
   471→    temp_diffs = DiffArtifacts(
   472→        raw_json_patch=raw_patch,
   473→        resolved_json_patch=resolved_patch,
   474→        includes_changes=includes_changes,
   475→        human=[]
   476→    )
   477→
   478→    # Generate human diff
   479→    human = format_human_diff(temp_diffs, base_version.config, draft_version.config,
   480→                              base_version.includes, draft_version.includes)
   481→
   482→    return DiffArtifacts(
   483→        raw_json_patch=raw_patch,
   484→        resolved_json_patch=resolved_patch,
   485→        includes_changes=includes_changes,
   486→        human=human
   487→    )
   488→
   489→
   490→# =============================================================================
   491→# Quorum Evaluation
   492→# =============================================================================
   493→
   494→def evaluate_quorum(proposal: Proposal) -> str:
   495→    """Evaluate the status of a proposal based on its reviews and policy."""
   496→    # Check for rejections first
   497→    if proposal.tally.rejections > 0:
   498→        return ProposalStatus.REJECTED
   499→
   500→    # Check if approved
   501→    if proposal.tally.approvals >= proposal.quorum.required_approvals:
   502→        return ProposalStatus.APPROVED
   503→
   504→    # Otherwise open
   505→    return ProposalStatus.OPEN
   506→
   507→
   508→def validate_review_against_policy(actor: str, decision: str,
   509→                                    proposal: Proposal) -> None:
   510→    """Validate that a review complies with the policy."""
   511→    # Check allowed_reviewers
   512→    if proposal.quorum.allowed_reviewers is not None:
   513→        if actor not in proposal.quorum.allowed_reviewers:
   514→            raise ConfigError('policy_violation',
   515→                            f'Actor {actor} is not in allowed_reviewers')
   516→
   517→    # Check author approval
   518→    if decision == 'approve' and actor == proposal.author:
   519→        if not proposal.quorum.allow_author_approval:
   520→            raise ConfigError('policy_violation',
   521→                            'Authors cannot approve their own proposals')
   522→
   523→
   524→# =============================================================================
   525→# Canonical JSON formatting constants
   526→CANONICAL_SEPARATORS = (',', ':')
   527→CANONICAL_INDENT = None  # Compact, but we'll add single \n at end
   528→
   529→
   530→# =============================================================================
   531→# Error handling
   532→# =============================================================================
   533→
   534→class ConfigError(Exception):
   535→    """Base exception for config service errors."""
   536→
   537→    def __init__(self, code: str, message: str, details: dict[str, Any] | None = None):
   538→        self.code = code
   539→        self.message = message
   540→        self.details = details or {}
   541→        super().__init__(message)
   542→
   543→
   544→Scope = dict[str, str]
   545→
   546→def validate_scope(d: dict[str, str]) -> dict[str, str]:
   547→    """Validate scope dict - returns the dict if valid."""
   548→    for k, v in d.items():
   549→        if not isinstance(k, str):
   550→            raise ConfigError('invalid_input', f'Scope key must be string, got {type(k).__name__}')
   551→        if not isinstance(v, str):
   552→            raise ConfigError('invalid_input', f'Scope value must be string, got {type(v).__name__}')
   553→    return d
   554→
   555→def scope_hash(scope: Scope) -> int:
   556→    """Hash a scope for dictionary lookup."""
   557→    return hash(tuple(sorted(scope.items())))
   558→
   559→
   560→# =============================================================================
   561→# Schema Data Models
   562→# =============================================================================
   563→
   564→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   565→class SchemaVersion:
   566→    """An immutable version of a JSON Schema."""
   567→    name: str
   568→    version: int
   569→    schema: dict[str, Any]  # The parsed JSON Schema object
   570→    raw_source: str | None = None  # Original raw string if provided
   571→
   572→    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   573→        return {
   574→            'name': self.name,
   575→            'version': self.version,
   576→            'schema': self.schema,
   577→        }
   578→
   579→
   580→class SchemaStorage:
   581→    """In-memory storage for schemas."""
   582→
   583→    def __init__(self):
   584→        # schema_name -> list of SchemaVersion sorted by version
   585→        self._schemas: dict[str, list[SchemaVersion]] = {}
   586→
   587→    def create_version(self, name: str, schema: dict[str, Any], raw_source: str | None = None) -> SchemaVersion:
   588→        """Create a new immutable schema version."""
   589→        if name not in self._schemas:
   590→            self._schemas[name] = []
   591→
   592→        versions = self._schemas[name]
   593→
   594→        # Check max versions
   595→        if len(versions) >= MAX_SCHEMA_VERSIONS_PER_NAME:
   596→            raise ConfigError('conflict', f'Maximum {MAX_SCHEMA_VERSIONS_PER_NAME} versions reached for schema {name}')
   597→
   598→        # Determine next version number
   599→        next_version = len(versions) + 1
   600→
   601→        new_schema = SchemaVersion(
   602→            name=name,
   603→            version=next_version,
   604→            schema=schema,
   605→            raw_source=raw_source
   606→        )
   607→
   608→        versions.append(new_schema)
   609→        return new_schema
   610→
   611→    def get_version(self, name: str, version: int) -> SchemaVersion:
   612→        """Get a specific schema version."""
   613→        if name not in self._schemas:
   614→            raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Schema {name} not found')
   615→
   616→        for sv in self._schemas[name]:
   617→            if sv.version == version:
   618→                return sv
   619→
   620→        raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Schema version {version} not found for {name}')
   621→
   622→    def list_versions(self, name: str) -> list[SchemaVersion]:
   623→        """List all versions for a schema name."""
   624→        if name not in self._schemas:
   625→            raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Schema {name} not found')
   626→        return list(self._schemas[name])
   627→
   628→    def get_latest(self, name: str) -> SchemaVersion | None:
   629→        """Get the latest schema version, or None if not found."""
   630→        if name not in self._schemas or not self._schemas[name]:
   631→            return None
   632→        return self._schemas[name][-1]
   633→
   634→
   635→# =============================================================================
   636→# Binding Data Models
   637→# =============================================================================
   638→
   639→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   640→class Binding:
   641→    """A binding associating a config identity with a schema."""
   642→    name: str
   643→    scope: Scope
   644→    schema_name: str
   645→    schema_version: int
   646→    active: bool = True
   647→
   648→    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   649→        return {
   650→            'name': self.name,
   651→            'scope': self.scope,
   652→            'schema_ref': {
   653→                'name': self.schema_name,
   654→                'version': self.schema_version,
   655→            },
   656→            'active': self.active,
   657→        }
   658→
   659→
   660→class BindingStorage:
   661→    """In-memory storage for schema bindings."""
   662→
   663→    def __init__(self):
   664→        # (name, scope_hash) -> Binding
   665→        self._bindings: dict[tuple[str, int], Binding] = {}
   666→
   667→    def _key(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> tuple[str, int]:
   668→        return (name, scope_hash(scope))
   669→
   670→    def bind(self, name: str, scope: Scope, schema_name: str, schema_version: int) -> Binding:
   671→        """Create or update a binding."""
   672→        key = self._key(name, scope)
   673→        binding = Binding(
   674→            name=name,
   675→            scope=scope,
   676→            schema_name=schema_name,
   677→            schema_version=schema_version,
   678→            active=True
   679→        )
   680→        self._bindings[key] = binding
   681→        return binding
   682→
   683→    def get_binding(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> Binding | None:
   684→        """Get the binding for a (name, scope) pair."""
   685→        key = self._key(name, scope)
   686→        return self._bindings.get(key)
   687→
   688→    def get_effective_schema(self, name: str, scope: Scope, schema_storage: SchemaStorage,
   689→                             override_schema_ref: dict[str, int] | None = None) -> tuple[SchemaVersion | None, dict[str, int] | None]:
   690→        """Get the effective schema for a config identity.
   691→
   692→        Returns a tuple of (schema_version, schema_ref_used).
   693→        If override_schema_ref is provided, use that.
   694→        Otherwise, use the active binding if any.
   695→        """
   696→        if override_schema_ref is not None:
   697→            schema_name = override_schema_ref.get('name')
   698→            schema_version_num = override_schema_ref.get('version')
   699→            if schema_name and schema_version_num is not None:
   700→                try:
   701→                    schema_ver = schema_storage.get_version(schema_name, schema_version_num)
   702→                    return schema_ver, override_schema_ref
   703→                except ConfigError:
   704→                    raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Schema {schema_name} version {schema_version_num} not found')
   705→
   706→        binding = self.get_binding(name, scope)
   707→        if binding is None:
   708→            return None, None
   709→
   710→        try:
   711→            schema_ver = schema_storage.get_version(binding.schema_name, binding.schema_version)
   712→            schema_ref = {'name': binding.schema_name, 'version': binding.schema_version}
   713→            return schema_ver, schema_ref
   714→        except ConfigError:
   715→            return None, None
   716→
   717→
   718→# =============================================================================
   719→# Raw Config Parsing
   720→# =============================================================================
   721→
   722→def _traverse_nested(obj, path, on_dict, on_list, on_scalar=None):
   723→    """Generic recursive traversal over nested dicts/lists.
   724→
   725→    on_dict: callable(dict_key, dict_value, current_path) -> None, or False to stop traversal
   726→    on_list: callable(index, item, current_path) -> None
   727→    on_scalar: callable(value, current_path) -> None, optional
   728→    """
   729→    if isinstance(obj, dict):
   730→        for key, value in obj.items():
   731→            cp = f'{path}/{key}' if path else f'/{key}'
   732→            if on_dict(key, value, cp) is False:
   733→                continue
   734→            _traverse_nested(value, cp, on_dict, on_list, on_scalar)
   735→    elif isinstance(obj, list):
   736→        for i, item in enumerate(obj):
   737→            cp = f'{path}/{i}'
   738→            on_list(i, item, cp)
   739→            _traverse_nested(item, cp, on_dict, on_list, on_scalar)
   740→    elif on_scalar:
   741→        on_scalar(obj, path)
   742→
   743→
   744→def check_merge_keys(obj, path=''):
   745→    """Check for YAML merge keys (<<) in parsed object."""
   746→    def on_dict(key, value, current_path):
   747→        if key == '<<':
   748→            raise ConfigError('unprocessable', 'YAML merge keys (<<) are not allowed',
   749→                            {'reason': 'yaml_feature_not_allowed'})
   750→    _traverse_nested(obj, path, on_dict, lambda i, item, p: None)
   751→
   752→
   753→def check_json_types(obj, path=''):
   754→    """Check for non-JSON types in TOML-parsed object."""
   755→    def on_scalar(value, current_path):
   756→        if value is None:
   757→            return
   758→        if not isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool)):
   759→            raise ConfigError('unprocessable',
   760→                            f'Non-JSON type {type(value).__name__} at {current_path} is not allowed in TOML configs. '
   761→                            f'Represent as strings if needed.',
   762→                            {'reason': 'non_json_type'})
   763→    _traverse_nested(obj, path, lambda k, v, p: None, lambda i, item, p: None, on_scalar)
   764→
   765→
   766→def check_for_external_refs(obj, path=''):
   767→    """Check for external $ref/$dynamicRef in schema."""
   768→    def on_dict(key, value, current_path):
   769→        if key == '$ref' and isinstance(value, str):
   770→            if value.startswith('http://') or value.startswith('https://'):
   771→                raise ConfigError('schema_invalid', 'External $ref not allowed',
   772→                                {'reason': 'external_ref_not_allowed'})
   773→        elif key == '$dynamicRef' and isinstance(value, str):
   774→            if value.startswith('http://') or value.startswith('https://'):
   775→                raise ConfigError('schema_invalid', 'External $dynamicRef not allowed',
   776→                                {'reason': 'external_ref_not_allowed'})
   777→    _traverse_nested(obj, path, on_dict, lambda i, item, p: None)
   778→
   779→
   780→def validate_yaml_features(raw_string: str, result: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   781→    """Validate YAML-specific features are not used."""
   782→    # Check for anchors/aliases in the raw string
   783→    if re.search(r'&[a-zA-Z0-9_]+|\*[a-zA-Z0-9_]+', raw_string):
   784→        raise ConfigError('unprocessable', 'YAML anchors/aliases are not allowed',
   785→                        {'reason': 'yaml_feature_not_allowed'})
   786→
   787→    # Check for custom tags
   788→    if re.search(r'!![a-zA-Z0-9_-]+', raw_string):
   789→        raise ConfigError('unprocessable', 'YAML custom tags are not allowed',
   790→                        {'reason': 'yaml_feature_not_allowed'})
   791→
   792→    check_merge_keys(result)
   793→
   794→
   795→def validate_toml_types(result: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   796→    """Validate TOML-parsed result contains only JSON-serializable types."""
   797→    check_json_types(result)
   798→
   799→
   800→def parse_raw_config(raw_string: str, raw_format: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
   801→    """Parse a raw config string in the specified format to a JSON object.
   802→
   803→    Args:
   804→        raw_string: The raw config string.
   805→        raw_format: One of 'json', 'yaml', 'toml'.
   806→
   807→    Returns:
   808→        The parsed JSON object (must be a dict).
   809→
   810→    Raises:
   811→        ConfigError: On parse errors or invalid formats.
   812→    """
   813→    if len(raw_string) > MAX_RAW_DATA_SIZE:
   814→        raise ConfigError('too_large', 'Raw config exceeds 1 MiB limit')
   815→
   816→    raw_string = raw_string.strip()
   817→
   818→    if raw_format == 'json':
   819→        try:
   820→            result = json.loads(raw_string)
   821→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   822→            raise ConfigError('unprocessable', f'Invalid JSON: {e}', {'reason': 'invalid_json'})
   823→
   824→    elif raw_format == 'yaml':
   825→        try:
   826→            result = yaml.safe_load(raw_string)
   827→        except yaml.YAMLError as e:
   828→            raise ConfigError('unprocessable', f'Invalid YAML: {e}', {'reason': 'invalid_yaml'})
   829→
   830→        validate_yaml_features(raw_string, result)
   831→
   832→    elif raw_format == 'toml':
   833→        try:
   834→            result = toml.loads(raw_string)
   835→        except toml.TomlDecodeError as e:
   836→            raise ConfigError('unprocessable', f'Invalid TOML: {e}', {'reason': 'invalid_toml'})
   837→
   838→        validate_toml_types(result)
   839→
   840→    else:
   841→        raise ConfigError('unsupported_format', f'Unsupported raw format: {raw_format}',
   842→                         {'supported_formats': ['json', 'yaml', 'toml']})
   843→
   844→    # The root must be a JSON object (dict), not array or scalar
   845→    if not isinstance(result, dict):
   846→        raise ConfigError('unprocessable',
   847→                         f'Config root must be a JSON object, got {type(result).__name__}',
   848→                         {'reason': 'root_not_object'})
   849→
   850→    return result
   851→
   852→
   853→def to_canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
   854→    """Convert object to canonical JSON string."""
   855→    # Sort keys recursively
   856→    def sort_keys(o):
   857→        if isinstance(o, dict):
   858→            return {k: sort_keys(v) for k, v in sorted(o.items())}
   859→        elif isinstance(o, list):
   860→            return [sort_keys(item) for item in o]
   861→        return o
   862→
   863→    sorted_obj = sort_keys(obj)
   864→    # Use separators without spaces, ensure no trailing whitespace
   865→    json_str = json.dumps(
   866→        sorted_obj,
   867→        separators=CANONICAL_SEPARATORS,
   868→        ensure_ascii=False,
   869→        default=lambda o: str(o) if isinstance(o, (set, tuple)) else o
   870→    )
   871→    # Add single newline at end
   872→    return json_str + '\n'
   873→
   874→
   875→# =============================================================================
   876→# JSON Schema Validation
   877→# =============================================================================
   878→
   879→def validate_schema_against_itself(schema_doc: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:
   880→    """Validate that a document is a valid JSON Schema Draft 2020-12."""
   881→    if not isinstance(schema_doc, dict):
   882→        return False, 'Schema must be a JSON object'
   883→
   884→    if '$ref' in schema_doc:
   885→        ref_value = schema_doc['$ref']
   886→        if isinstance(ref_value, str) and (ref_value.startswith('http://') or ref_value.startswith('https://')):
   887→            return False, 'External $ref not allowed'
   888→
   889→    if '$dynamicRef' in schema_doc:
   890→        ref_value = schema_doc.get('$dynamicRef', '')
   891→        if isinstance(ref_value, str) and (ref_value.startswith('http://') or ref_value.startswith('https://')):
   892→            return False, 'External $dynamicRef not allowed'
   893→
   894→    return True, None
   895→
   896→
   897→def validate_against_schema(instance: dict[str, Any], schema_doc: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[bool, dict[str, str] | None]:
   898→    """Validate an instance against a JSON Schema."""
   899→    try:
   900→        validator = Draft202012Validator(schema_doc)
   901→        errors = list(validator.iter_errors(instance))
   902→        if not errors:
   903→            return True, None
   904→
   905→        best_error = None
   906→        best_path = None
   907→
   908→        for error in errors:
   909→            path = error.json_path or '/'
   910→            rule = error.validator
   911→            details = {'path': path, 'rule': rule}
   912→
   913→            if rule == 'type':
   914→                expected = error.validator_value
   915→                expected = expected[0] if isinstance(expected, list) and expected else str(expected)
   916→                details['expected'] = str(expected)
   917→                details['actual'] = type(error.instance).__name__
   918→            elif rule == 'enum':
   919→                details['expected'] = 'one of ' + str(error.validator_value)
   920→                details['actual'] = str(error.instance)
   921→            elif rule == 'required':
   922→                details['expected'] = 'required property ' + str(error.validator_value)
   923→                details['actual'] = 'missing'
   924→            elif rule == 'pattern':
   925→                details['expected'] = f'pattern {error.validator_value}'
   926→                details['actual'] = str(error.instance)[:50] + ('...' if len(str(error.instance)) > 50 else '')
   927→            elif rule in ('minimum', 'maximum', 'multipleOf'):
   928→                details['expected'] = f'{rule} {error.validator_value}'
   929→                details['actual'] = str(error.instance)
   930→            elif rule in ('minLength', 'maxLength'):
   931→                details['expected'] = f'{rule} {error.validator_value}'
   932→                details['actual'] = f'length {len(str(error.instance))}'
   933→            elif rule in ('minProperties', 'maxProperties'):
   934→                details['expected'] = f'{rule} {error.validator_value}'
   935→                details['actual'] = f'{len(error.instance)} properties'
   936→            else:
   937→                details['expected'] = str(error.validator_value)[:100]
   938→                details['actual'] = str(error.instance)[:100]
   939→
   940→            if best_path is None or path < best_path:
   941→                best_path = path
   942→                best_error = details
   943→
   944→        return False, best_error
   945→    except Exception as e:
   946→        return False, {'path': '/', 'rule': 'schema_error', 'expected': 'valid JSON Schema', 'actual': str(e)}
   947→
   948→
   949→# =============================================================================
   950→# Existing Data Models (from Part 1, kept for compatibility)
   951→# =============================================================================
   952→
   953→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   954→class IncludeRef:
   955→    """Reference to another config."""
   956→
   957→    name: str
   958→    scope: Scope
   959→    version: int | None  # None means use current active
   960→
   961→    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   962→        result = {
   963→            'name': self.name,
   964→            'scope': self.scope,
   965→        }
   966→        if self.version is not None:
   967→            result['version'] = self.version
   968→        return result
   969→
   970→    @classmethod
   971→    def from_dict(cls, d: dict[str, Any]) -> 'IncludeRef':
   972→        if not isinstance(d, dict):
   973→            raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Include reference must be a JSON object')
   974→
   975→        if 'name' not in d:
   976→            raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Include reference must have "name"')
   977→        if 'scope' not in d:
   978→            raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Include reference must have "scope"')
   979→
   980→        name = d['name']
   981→        if not isinstance(name, str) or not name:
   982→            raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Include name must be non-empty string')
   983→
   984→        scope = validate_scope(d['scope'])
   985→        version = d.get('version')
   986→        if version is not None:
   987→            if not isinstance(version, int) or version < 1:
   988→                raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Version must be positive integer')
   989→
   990→        return cls(name=name, scope=scope, version=version)
   991→
   992→
   993→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   994→class ConfigVersion:
   995→    """An immutable version of a config."""
   996→
   997→    name: str
   998→    scope: Scope
   999→    version: int
  1000→    config: dict[str, Any]
  1001→    includes: tuple[IncludeRef, ...] = ()
  1002→    active: bool = False
  1003→    status: str = 'draft'  # 'draft' or 'active'
  1004→
  1005→    def to_dict(self, include_config: bool = True) -> dict[str, Any]:
  1006→        result = {
  1007→            'name': self.name,
  1008→            'scope': self.scope,
  1009→            'version': self.version,
  1010→            'status': self.status,
  1011→            'active': self.active,
  1012→        }
  1013→        if include_config:
  1014→            result['config'] = self.config
  1015→            result['includes'] = [inc.to_dict() for inc in self.includes]
  1016→        return result
  1017→
  1018→
  1019→# =============================================================================
  1020→# Storage (from Part 1, extended for bindings)
  1021→# =============================================================================
  1022→
  1023→@dataclass
  1024→class ConfigStorage:
  1025→    """In-memory storage for configs. In production, would use a database."""
  1026→
  1027→    # (name, scope_hash) -> list of ConfigVersion sorted by version
  1028→    _configs: dict[tuple[str, int], list[ConfigVersion]] = field(default_factory=dict)
  1029→
  1030→    def _scope_key(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> tuple[str, int]:
  1031→        """Generate a key for the (name, scope) pair."""
  1032→        return (name, scope_hash(scope))
  1033→
  1034→    def _get_versions(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> list[ConfigVersion]:
  1035→        """Get all versions for a (name, scope) pair."""
  1036→        key = self._scope_key(name, scope)
  1037→        return self._configs.get(key, [])
  1038→
  1039→    def create_version(
  1040→        self,
  1041→        name: str,
  1042→        scope: Scope,
  1043→        config: dict[str, Any],
  1044→        includes: list[IncludeRef],
  1045→        inherits_active: bool = False
  1046→    ) -> ConfigVersion:
  1047→        """Create a new immutable version."""
  1048→        key = self._scope_key(name, scope)
  1049→        versions = self._configs.get(key, [])
  1050→
  1051→        # Check max versions
  1052→        if len(versions) >= MAX_VERSIONS_PER_SCOPE:
  1053→            raise ConfigError('conflict', f'Maximum {MAX_VERSIONS_PER_SCOPE} versions reached for {name}')
  1054→
  1055→        # Determine next version number
  1056→        next_version = len(versions) + 1
  1057→
  1058→        # Check idempotency - if identical request, return existing
  1059→        for v in versions:
  1060→            if v.config == config and v.includes == tuple(includes):
  1061→                # Return existing version, but ensure it's active
  1062→                if not v.active:
  1063→                    object.__setattr__(v, 'active', True)
  1064→                return v
  1065→
  1066→        # If inherits_active is True, inherit omitted fields from active version
  1067→        if inherits_active and versions:
  1068→            active = versions[-1]  # Last one is active
  1069→            config = deep_merge(active.config, config)
  1070→
  1071→        # Deactivate all existing versions
  1072→        for v in versions:
  1073→            object.__setattr__(v, 'active', False)
  1074→
  1075→        # Create new version
  1076→        new_version = ConfigVersion(
  1077→            name=name,
  1078→            scope=scope,
  1079→            version=next_version,
  1080→            config=config,
  1081→            includes=tuple(includes),
  1082→            active=False,
  1083→            status='draft'
  1084→        )
  1085→
  1086→        self._configs[key] = versions + [new_version]
  1087→        return new_version
  1088→
  1089→    def get_version(self, name: str, scope: Scope, version: int) -> ConfigVersion:
  1090→        """Get a specific version."""
  1091→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
  1092→        for v in versions:
  1093→            if v.version == version:
  1094→                return v
  1095→        raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Version {version} not found for {name}')
  1096→
  1097→    def get_active(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> ConfigVersion:
  1098→        """Get the active version."""
  1099→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
  1100→        for v in reversed(versions):
  1101→            if v.active:
  1102→                return v
  1103→        raise ConfigError('not_found', f'No active version for {name}')
  1104→
  1105→    def list_versions(self, name: str, scope: Scope) -> list[ConfigVersion]:
  1106→        """List all versions for a (name, scope) pair."""
  1107→        return list(self._get_versions(name, scope))
  1108→
  1109→    def activate_version(self, name: str, scope: Scope, version: int) -> ConfigVersion:
  1110→        """Activate a specific version."""
  1111→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
  1112→        for v in versions:
  1113→            if v.version == version:
  1114→                for ver in versions:
  1115→                    object.__setattr__(ver, 'active', ver.version == version)
  1116→                return v
  1117→        raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Version {version} not found for {name}')
  1118→
  1119→    def rollback(self, name: str, scope: Scope, to_version: int) -> ConfigVersion:
  1120→        """Rollback to an earlier version."""
  1121→        versions = self._get_versions(name, scope)
  1122→        target = None
  1123→        for v in versions:
  1124→            if v.version == to_version:
  1125→                target = v
  1126→                break
  1127→        if target is None:
  1128→            raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Version {to_version} not found for {name}')
  1129→
  1130→        current_active = None
  1131→        for v in reversed(versions):
  1132→            if v.active:
  1133→                current_active = v
  1134→                break
  1135→        if current_active is None:
  1136→            raise ConfigError('not_found', f'No active version for {name}')
  1137→
  1138→        if to_version > current_active.version:
  1139→            raise ConfigError('conflict', f'Cannot rollback to version {to_version} (newer than active {current_active.version})')
  1140→
  1141→        return self.activate_version(name, scope, to_version)
  1142→
  1143→
  1144→# =============================================================================
  1145→# Deep merge with type checking
  1146→# =============================================================================
  1147→
  1148→def deep_merge(base: dict[str, Any], override: dict[str, Any], path: str = '') -> dict[str, Any]:
  1149→    """Deep merge two dictionaries. Override values take precedence."""
  1150→    result = dict(base)
  1151→
  1152→    for key, value in override.items():
  1153→        current_path = f'{path}/{key}' if path else f'/{key}'
  1154→
  1155→        if key in result:
  1156→            existing = result[key]
  1157→
  1158→            if isinstance(existing, dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
  1159→                result[key] = deep_merge(existing, value, current_path)
  1160→            elif isinstance(existing, list) and isinstance(value, list):
  1161→                result[key] = value
  1162→            elif isinstance(existing, (str, int, float, bool, type(None))) and \
  1163→                 isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool, type(None))):
  1164→                result[key] = value
  1165→            else:
  1166→                raise ConfigError(
  1167→                    'unprocessable',
  1168→                    f'Type conflict at {current_path}: {type(existing).__name__} vs {type(value).__name__}',
  1169→                    {'path': current_path}
  1170→                )
  1171→        else:
  1172→            result[key] = value
  1173→
  1174→    return result
  1175→
  1176→
  1177→# =============================================================================
  1178→# Resolution engine
  1179→# =============================================================================
  1180→
  1181→@dataclass
  1182→class ResolutionNode:
  1183→    """A node in the resolution graph."""
  1184→
  1185→    name: str
  1186→    scope: Scope
  1187→    version_used: int
  1188→
  1189→    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
  1190→        return {
  1191→            'name': self.name,
  1192→            'scope': self.scope,
  1193→            'version_used': self.version_used,
  1194→        }
  1195→
  1196→
  1197→def resolve_config(
  1198→    storage: ConfigStorage,
  1199→    name: str,
  1200→    scope: Scope,
  1201→    version: int | None,
  1202→    visited: set[tuple[str, int, int]] | None = None
  1203→) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], list[ResolutionNode]]:
  1204→    """Resolve a config with all includes applied."""
  1205→    if visited is None:
  1206→        visited = set()
  1207→
  1208→    if len(visited) > MAX_INCLUDE_CHAIN:
  1209→        raise ConfigError('unprocessable', 'Maximum include chain length exceeded', {'reason': 'max_depth'})
  1210→
  1211→    if version is None:
  1212→        config_version = storage.get_active(name, scope)
  1213→    else:
  1214→        config_version = storage.get_version(name, scope, version)
  1215→
  1216→    config_id = (name, hash(scope), config_version.version)
  1217→
  1218→    if config_id in visited:
  1219→        raise ConfigError('cycle_detected', f'Cycle detected involving {name}', {'cycle': [n.to_dict() for n in visited]})
  1220→
  1221→    visited = visited | {config_id}
  1222→
  1223→    resolved = {}
  1224→    graph = [ResolutionNode(name=config_version.name, scope=config_version.scope, version_used=config_version.version)]
  1225→
  1226→    for include_ref in config_version.includes:
  1227→        ref_version = include_ref.version
  1228→        if ref_version is None:
  1229→            ref_version = storage.get_active(include_ref.name, include_ref.scope).version
  1230→
  1231→        included_config, included_graph = resolve_config(storage, include_ref.name, include_ref.scope, ref_version, visited)
  1232→
  1233→        try:
  1234→            resolved = deep_merge(resolved, included_config)
  1235→        except ConfigError as e:
  1236→            if e.code == 'unprocessable':
  1237→                raise
  1238→            raise
  1239→
  1240→        for node in included_graph:
  1241→            node_id = (node.name, hash(node.scope), node.version_used)
  1242→            if node_id not in {(n.name, hash(n.scope), n.version_used) for n in graph}:
  1243→                graph.append(node)
  1244→
  1245→    try:
  1246→        resolved = deep_merge(resolved, config_version.config)
  1247→    except ConfigError:
  1248→        raise
  1249→
  1250→    return resolved, graph
  1251→
  1252→

  1506→# =============================================================================
  1507→# Request helpers
  1508→# =============================================================================
  1509→
  1510→async def _read_body(request: Request) -> dict[str, Any]:
  1511→    """Read and parse request body."""
  1512→    body = await request.body()
  1513→    if not body:
  1514→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Request body is required')
  1515→    try:
  1516→        return json.loads(body)
  1517→    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
  1518→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', f'Invalid JSON: {e}')
  1519→
  1520→
  1521→# =============================================================================
  1522→# Error response helper
  1523→# =============================================================================
  1524→
  1525→def error_response(code: str, message: str, details: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> JSONResponse:
  1526→    """Create a standardized error response."""
  1527→    status_map = {
  1528→        'invalid_input': 400,
  1529→        'not_found': 404,
  1530→        'conflict': 409,
  1531→        'cycle_detected': 409,
  1532→        'approval_required': 409,
  1533→        'stale_base': 409,
  1534→        'not_mergeable': 409,
  1535→        'unprocessable': 422,
  1536→        'rate_limited': 429,
  1537→        'too_large': 413,
  1538→        'unsupported_format': 415,
  1539→        'schema_invalid': 422,
  1540→        'validation_failed': 422,
  1541→        'policy_violation': 422,
  1542→        'internal': 500,
  1543→    }
  1544→    status_code = status_map.get(code, 500)
  1545→
  1546→    return JSONResponse(
  1547→        status_code=status_code,
  1548→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1549→            'error': {
  1550→                'code': code,
  1551→                'message': message,
  1552→                'details': details or {},
  1553→            }
  1554→        }),
  1555→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1556→    )
  1557→
  1558→
  1559→# =============================================================================
  1560→# Exception handlers
  1561→# =============================================================================
  1562→
  1563→@app.exception_handler(ConfigError)
  1564→async def config_error_handler(request: Request, exc: ConfigError):
  1565→    return error_response(exc.code, exc.message, exc.details)
  1566→
  1567→
  1568→@app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError)
  1569→async def validation_error_handler(request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError):
  1570→    # Extract error details
  1571→    errors = exc.errors()
  1572→    details = {'errors': errors}
  1573→    return error_response('invalid_input', 'Validation failed', details)
  1574→
  1575→
  1576→@app.exception_handler(HTTPException)
  1577→async def http_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: HTTPException):
  1578→    if exc.status_code == 413:
  1579→        return error_response('too_large', 'Request body too large')
  1580→    return error_response('internal', str(exc.detail), {'status_code': exc.status_code})
  1581→
  1582→
  1583→# =============================================================================
  1584→# Middleware
  1585→# =============================================================================
  1586→
  1587→@app.middleware('http')
  1588→async def request_size_limit(request: Request, call_next):
  1589→    """Middleware to enforce max request size."""
  1590→    content_length = request.headers.get('content-length')
  1591→    if content_length and int(content_length) > MAX_REQUEST_SIZE:
  1592→        return error_response('too_large', 'Request body exceeds 1 MiB limit')
  1593→
  1594→    # Also check actual body size
  1595→    body = await request.body()
  1596→    if len(body) > MAX_REQUEST_SIZE:
  1597→        return error_response('too_large', 'Request body exceeds 1 MiB limit')
  1598→
  1599→    # Restore body for subsequent reading
  1600→    request._body = body
  1601→    return await call_next(request)
  1602→

  1614→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}')
  1615→async def create_config(request: Request, name: str):
  1616→    """Create a new version of a config."""
  1617→    body_data = await request.body()
  1618→    if not body_data:
  1619→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Request body is required')
  1620→
  1621→    try:
  1622→        data = json.loads(body_data)
  1623→    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
  1624→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', f'Invalid JSON: {e}')
  1625→
  1626→    # Check if using extended format (raw_config or schema_ref)
  1627→    use_extended = 'raw_config' in data or 'schema_ref' in data
  1628→
  1629→    if use_extended:
  1630→        body = CreateConfigRequestExtended.model_validate(data)
  1631→        scope = body.scope
  1632→        includes = [IncludeRef.from_dict(inc.model_dump()) for inc in body.includes]
  1633→        inherits_active = body.inherits_active
  1634→        schema_ref_override = body.schema_ref
  1635→
  1636→        # Parse config content
  1637→        if body.config is not None:
  1638→            config = body.config
  1639→        else:
  1640→            raw_config = body.raw_config
  1641→            raw_format = body.raw_format or 'json'
  1642→            if not isinstance(raw_config, str):
  1643→                raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'raw_config must be a string')
  1644→            config = parse_raw_config(raw_config, raw_format)
  1645→    else:
  1646→        body = CreateConfigRequest.model_validate(data)
  1647→        scope = body.scope
  1648→        config = body.config
  1649→        includes = [IncludeRef.from_dict(inc) for inc in body.includes]
  1650→        inherits_active = body.inherits_active
  1651→        schema_ref_override = None
  1652→
  1653→    # Validate config is a dict
  1654→    if not isinstance(config, dict):
  1655→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Config must be a JSON object')
  1656→
  1657→    # Determine effective schema
  1658→    schema_version, _ = binding_storage.get_effective_schema(name, scope, schema_storage, schema_ref_override)
  1659→
  1660→    # Validate against schema if one exists
  1661→    if schema_version is not None:
  1662→        is_valid, error_details = validate_against_schema(config, schema_version.schema)
  1663→        if not is_valid:
  1664→            raise ConfigError('validation_failed', 'Config does not conform to schema', error_details)
  1665→
  1666→    new_version = storage.create_version(name, scope, config, includes, inherits_active)
  1667→
  1668→    return JSONResponse(
  1669→        status_code=201,
  1670→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1671→            'name': new_version.name,
  1672→            'scope': new_version.scope,
  1673→            'version': new_version.version,
  1674→            'active': new_version.active,
  1675→        }),
  1676→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1677→    )
  1678→
  1679→
  1680→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}:versions')
  1681→async def list_versions(request: Request, name: str):
  1682→    """List all versions for a (name, scope) pair."""
  1683→    data = await _read_body(request)
  1684→
  1685→    if 'scope' not in data:
  1686→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: scope')
  1687→
  1688→    scope = validate_scope(data['scope'])
  1689→    versions = storage.list_versions(name, scope)
  1690→
  1691→    versions.sort(key=lambda v: v.version)
  1692→    return JSONResponse(
  1693→        status_code=200,
  1694→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1695→            'name': name,
  1696→            'scope': scope,
  1697→            'versions': [{'version': v.version, 'active': v.active} for v in versions]
  1698→        }),
  1699→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1700→    )
  1701→
  1702→
  1703→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}/{version}')
  1704→async def get_version(request: Request, name: str, version: int):
  1705→    """Get a specific raw version."""
  1706→    data = await _read_body(request)
  1707→
  1708→    if 'scope' not in data:
  1709→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: scope')
  1710→
  1711→    config_version = storage.get_version(name, validate_scope(data['scope']), version)
  1712→
  1713→    return JSONResponse(
  1714→        status_code=200,
  1715→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1716→            'name': config_version.name,
  1717→            'scope': config_version.scope,
  1718→            'version': config_version.version,
  1719→            'active': config_version.active,
  1720→            'config': config_version.config,
  1721→            'includes': [inc.to_dict() for inc in config_version.includes]
  1722→        }),
  1723→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1724→    )
  1725→
  1726→
  1727→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}:active')
  1728→async def get_active(request: Request, name: str):
  1729→    """Get the active raw version."""
  1730→    data = await _read_body(request)
  1731→
  1732→    if 'scope' not in data:
  1733→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: scope')
  1734→
  1735→    config_version = storage.get_active(name, validate_scope(data['scope']))
  1736→
  1737→    return JSONResponse(
  1738→        status_code=200,
  1739→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1740→            'name': config_version.name,
  1741→            'scope': config_version.scope,
  1742→            'version': config_version.version,
  1743→            'active': config_version.active,
  1744→            'config': config_version.config,
  1745→            'includes': [inc.to_dict() for inc in config_version.includes]
  1746→        }),
  1747→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1748→    )
  1749→
  1750→
  1751→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}/{version}:activate')
  1752→async def activate_version(request: Request, name: str, version: int):
  1753→    """Activate a specific version."""
  1754→    data = await _read_body(request)
  1755→
  1756→    if 'scope' not in data:
  1757→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: scope')
  1758→
  1759→    activated = storage.activate_version(name, validate_scope(data['scope']), version)
  1760→
  1761→    return JSONResponse(
  1762→        status_code=200,
  1763→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1764→            'name': activated.name,
  1765→            'scope': activated.scope,
  1766→            'version': activated.version,
  1767→            'active': activated.active
  1768→        }),
  1769→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1770→    )
  1771→
  1772→
  1773→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}:rollback')
  1774→async def rollback(request: Request, name: str):
  1775→    """Rollback to an earlier version."""
  1776→    data = await _read_body(request)
  1777→
  1778→    if 'scope' not in data:
  1779→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: scope')
  1780→    if 'to_version' not in data:
  1781→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: to_version')
  1782→
  1783→    to_version = data['to_version']
  1784→    if not isinstance(to_version, int) or to_version < 1:
  1785→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'to_version must be a positive integer')
  1786→
  1787→    rolled_back = storage.rollback(name, validate_scope(data['scope']), to_version)
  1788→
  1789→    return JSONResponse(
  1790→        status_code=200,
  1791→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1792→            'name': rolled_back.name,
  1793→            'scope': rolled_back.scope,
  1794→            'version': rolled_back.version,
  1795→            'active': rolled_back.active
  1796→        }),
  1797→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1798→    )
  1799→
  1800→
  1801→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}:resolve')
  1802→async def resolve(request: Request, name: str):
  1803→    """Resolve a config with all imports applied, with optional validation."""
  1804→    data = await _read_body(request)
  1805→
  1806→    if 'scope' not in data:
  1807→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: scope')
  1808→
  1809→    body = ResolveRequest.model_validate(data)
  1810→    scope = body.scope
  1811→    version = body.version
  1812→    schema_ref_override = body.schema_ref
  1813→
  1814→    # Perform resolution
  1815→    resolved_config, resolution_graph = resolve_config(storage, name, scope, version)
  1816→
  1817→    # Determine version_used
  1818→    if version is None:
  1819→        config_version = storage.get_active(name, scope)
  1820→        version_used = config_version.version
  1821→    else:
  1822→        version_used = version
  1823→
  1824→    # Determine effective schema for validation
  1825→    schema_version, schema_ref = binding_storage.get_effective_schema(
  1826→        name, scope, schema_storage, schema_ref_override
  1827→    )
  1828→
  1829→    validated_against = None
  1830→    if schema_version is not None:
  1831→        is_valid, error_details = validate_against_schema(resolved_config, schema_version.schema)
  1832→        if not is_valid:
  1833→            raise ConfigError('validation_failed', 'Config does not conform to schema', error_details)
  1834→        validated_against = schema_ref
  1835→
  1836→    response = {
  1837→        'name': name,
  1838→        'scope': scope,
  1839→        'version_used': version_used,
  1840→        'resolved_config': resolved_config,
  1841→        'resolution_graph': [node.to_dict() for node in resolution_graph],
  1842→    }
  1843→    if validated_against is not None:
  1844→        response['validated_against'] = validated_against
  1845→
  1846→    return JSONResponse(
  1847→        status_code=200,
  1848→        content=to_canonical_json(response),
  1849→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1850→    )
  1851→
  1852→
  1853→# =============================================================================
  1854→# NEW: Schema endpoints
  1855→# =============================================================================
  1856→
  1857→@app.post('/v1/schemas/{schema_name}')
  1858→async def create_schema(request: Request, schema_name: str):
  1859→    """Create a new version of a JSON Schema."""
  1860→    body_data = await _read_body(request)
  1861→
  1862→    schema_doc = None
  1863→
  1864→    # Check for structured JSON schema (preferred)
  1865→    if 'schema' in body_data:
  1866→        schema_doc = body_data['schema']
  1867→        if not isinstance(schema_doc, dict):
  1868→            raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Schema must be a JSON object')
  1869→        raw_source = None
  1870→
  1871→    # Or raw schema
  1872→    elif 'raw_schema' in body_data:
  1873→        raw_schema = body_data['raw_schema']
  1874→        raw_format = body_data.get('raw_format', 'json')
  1875→
  1876→        if not isinstance(raw_schema, str):
  1877→            raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'raw_schema must be a string')
  1878→
  1879→        if raw_format == 'json':
  1880→            try:
  1881→                schema_doc = json.loads(raw_schema)
  1882→            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
  1883→                raise ConfigError('schema_invalid', f'Invalid JSON schema: {e}', {'reason': 'invalid_json'})
  1884→        elif raw_format == 'yaml':
  1885→            try:
  1886→                schema_doc = yaml.safe_load(raw_schema)
  1887→            except yaml.YAMLError as e:
  1888→                raise ConfigError('schema_invalid', f'Invalid YAML schema: {e}', {'reason': 'invalid_yaml'})
  1889→        else:
  1890→            raise ConfigError('unsupported_format', f'Unsupported raw format: {raw_format}')
  1891→
  1892→        raw_source = raw_schema
  1893→
  1894→    else:
  1895→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Must provide either "schema" or "raw_schema"')
  1896→
  1897→    # Validate it's a valid JSON Schema 2020-12
  1898→    is_valid, error_msg = validate_schema_against_itself(schema_doc)
  1899→    if not is_valid:
  1900→        raise ConfigError('schema_invalid', error_msg, {'reason': 'invalid_schema'})
  1901→
  1902→    # Additional check: no external $ref
  1903→    try:
  1904→        check_for_external_refs(schema_doc)
  1905→    except ConfigError:
  1906→        raise
  1907→
  1908→    # Create schema version
  1909→    new_schema = schema_storage.create_version(schema_name, schema_doc, raw_source)
  1910→
  1911→    return JSONResponse(
  1912→        status_code=201,
  1913→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1914→            'name': new_schema.name,
  1915→            'version': new_schema.version,
  1916→        }),
  1917→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1918→    )
  1919→
  1920→
  1921→@app.post('/v1/schemas/{schema_name}/versions')
  1922→async def list_schema_versions(request: Request, schema_name: str):
  1923→    """List all versions of a schema."""
  1924→    body_data = await _read_body(request)
  1925→    # Body can be empty or {}
  1926→
  1927→    versions = schema_storage.list_versions(schema_name)
  1928→    versions.sort(key=lambda v: v.version)
  1929→
  1930→    return JSONResponse(
  1931→        status_code=200,
  1932→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1933→            'name': schema_name,
  1934→            'versions': [{'version': v.version} for v in versions]
  1935→        }),
  1936→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1937→    )
  1938→
  1939→
  1940→@app.post('/v1/schemas/{schema_name}/{schema_version}')
  1941→async def get_schema(request: Request, schema_name: str, schema_version: int):
  1942→    """Get a specific schema version."""
  1943→    body_data = await _read_body(request)
  1944→    # Body can be empty or {}
  1945→
  1946→    schema_ver = schema_storage.get_version(schema_name, schema_version)
  1947→
  1948→    return JSONResponse(
  1949→        status_code=200,
  1950→        content=to_canonical_json({
  1951→            'name': schema_ver.name,
  1952→            'version': schema_ver.version,
  1953→            'schema': schema_ver.schema,
  1954→        }),
  1955→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  1956→    )
  1957→
  1958→
  1959→# =============================================================================
  1960→# NEW: Binding endpoints
  1961→# =============================================================================
  1962→
  1963→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}:bind')
  1964→async def bind_schema(request: Request, name: str):
  1965→    """Bind a schema to a config identity."""
  1966→    body_data = await _read_body(request)
  1967→
  1968→    if 'scope' not in body_data:
  1969→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: scope')
  1970→    if 'schema_ref' not in body_data:
  1971→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: schema_ref')
  1972→
  1973→    scope = validate_scope(body_data['scope'])
  1974→    schema_ref = body_data['schema_ref']
  1975→
  1976→    if not isinstance(schema_ref, dict):
  1977→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'schema_ref must be an object')
  1978→    if 'name' not in schema_ref or 'version' not in schema_ref:
  1979→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'schema_ref must have "name" and "version"')
  1980→
  1981→    schema_name = schema_ref['name']
  1982→    schema_version_num = schema_ref['version']
  1983→
  1984→    if not isinstance(schema_name, str):
  1985→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'schema_ref.name must be a string')
  1986→    if not isinstance(schema_version_num, int) or schema_version_num < 1:
  1987→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'schema_ref.version must be a positive integer')
  1988→
  1989→    # Check that the schema exists
  1990→    try:
  1991→        schema_storage.get_version(schema_name, schema_version_num)
  1992→    except ConfigError as e:
  1993→        if e.code == 'not_found':
  1994→            raise ConfigError('conflict', f'Schema {schema_name} version {schema_version_num} does not exist')
  1995→        raise
  1996→
  1997→    # Create or update binding
  1998→    binding = binding_storage.bind(name, scope, schema_name, schema_version_num)
  1999→
  2000→    return JSONResponse(
  2001→        status_code=200,
  2002→        content=to_canonical_json({
  2003→            'name': binding.name,
  2004→            'scope': binding.scope,
  2005→            'schema_ref': {
  2006→                'name': binding.schema_name,
  2007→                'version': binding.schema_version,
  2008→            },
  2009→            'active': binding.active,
  2010→        }),
  2011→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  2012→    )
  2013→
  2014→
  2015→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}/schema')
  2016→async def get_binding(request: Request, name: str):
  2017→    """Read the active binding for a config identity."""
  2018→    body_data = await _read_body(request)
  2019→
  2020→    if 'scope' not in body_data:
  2021→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: scope')
  2022→
  2023→    scope = validate_scope(body_data['scope'])
  2024→
  2025→    binding = binding_storage.get_binding(name, scope)
  2026→
  2027→    if binding is None:
  2028→        raise ConfigError('not_found', f'No binding found for {name} with scope {scope}')
  2029→
  2030→    return JSONResponse(
  2031→        status_code=200,
  2032→        content=to_canonical_json({
  2033→            'name': binding.name,
  2034→            'scope': binding.scope,
  2035→            'schema_ref': {
  2036→                'name': binding.schema_name,
  2037→                'version': binding.schema_version,
  2038→            },
  2039→        }),
  2040→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  2041→    )
  2042→
  2043→
  2044→# =============================================================================
  2045→# NEW: Validate-only endpoint
  2046→# =============================================================================
  2047→
  2048→@app.post('/v1/configs/{name}:validate')
  2049→async def validate_config(request: Request, name: str):
  2050→    """Validate a config version or resolved config against a schema."""
  2051→    body_data = await _read_body(request)
  2052→
  2053→    if 'scope' not in body_data:
  2054→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Missing required field: scope')
  2055→
  2056→    scope = validate_scope(body_data['scope'])
  2057→    version = body_data.get('version')
  2058→    schema_ref_override = body_data.get('schema_ref')
  2059→    mode = body_data.get('mode', 'resolved')
  2060→
  2061→    if mode not in ('stored', 'resolved'):
  2062→        raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'mode must be "stored" or "resolved"')
  2063→
  2064→    if version is not None:
  2065→        if not isinstance(version, int) or version < 1:
  2066→            raise ConfigError('invalid_input', 'Version must be a positive integer')
  2067→
  2068→    # Determine which config to validate
  2069→    if mode == 'stored':
  2070→        # Get the stored config
  2071→        try:
  2072→            if version is None:
  2073→                config_version = storage.get_active(name, scope)
  2074→            else:
  2075→                config_version = storage.get_version(name, scope, version)
  2076→
  2077→            config_to_validate = config_version.config
  2078→            version_used = config_version.version
  2079→        except ConfigError as e:
  2080→            if e.code == 'not_found':
  2081→                raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Config version not found for {name}')
  2082→            raise
  2083→    else:
  2084→        # mode == 'resolved': resolve and validate the resolved config
  2085→        try:
  2086→            resolved_config, _ = resolve_config(storage, name, scope, version)
  2087→            # Determine version_used
  2088→            if version is None:
  2089→                config_version = storage.get_active(name, scope)
  2090→                version_used = config_version.version
  2091→            else:
  2092→                version_used = version
  2093→            config_to_validate = resolved_config
  2094→        except ConfigError as e:
  2095→            if e.code == 'not_found':
  2096→                raise ConfigError('not_found', f'Config version not found for {name}')
  2097→            raise
  2098→
  2099→    # Determine effective schema
  2100→    schema_version, schema_ref = binding_storage.get_effective_schema(
  2101→        name, scope, schema_storage, schema_ref_override
  2102→    )
  2103→
  2104→    if schema_version is None:
  2105→        # No schema - still return valid but with null validated_against
  2106→        return JSONResponse(
  2107→            status_code=200,
  2108→            content=to_canonical_json({
  2109→                'name': name,
  2110→                'scope': scope,
  2111→                'version_used': version_used,
  2112→                'mode': mode,
  2113→                'valid': True,
  2114→                'validated_against': None,
  2115→            }),
  2116→            media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  2117→        )
  2118→
  2119→    # Validate
  2120→    is_valid, error_details = validate_against_schema(config_to_validate, schema_version.schema)
  2121→
  2122→    if not is_valid:
  2123→        raise ConfigError('validation_failed', 'Config does not conform to schema', error_details)
  2124→
  2125→    return JSONResponse(
  2126→        status_code=200,
  2127→        content=to_canonical_json({
  2128→            'name': name,
  2129→            'scope': scope,
  2130→            'version_used': version_used,
  2131→            'mode': mode,
  2132→            'valid': True,
  2133→            'validated_against': schema_ref,
  2134→        }),
  2135→        media_type='application/json; charset=utf-8'
  2136→    )
  2137→
  2138→
  2139→# =============================================================================
  2140→# Main entry point
  2141→# =============================================================================
  2142→
  2143→def main():
  2144→    import argparse
  2145→
  2146→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Config Service')
  2147→    parser.add_argument('--address', default='0.0.0.0', help='Address to bind to')
  2148→    parser.add_argument('--port', type=int, default=8080, help='Port to listen on')
  2149→    args = parser.parse_args()
  2150→
  2151→    uvicorn.run(
  2152→        'config_server:app',
  2153→        host=args.address,
  2154→        port=args.port,
  2155→        log_level='info'
  2156→    )
  2157→
  2158→
  2159→if __name__ == '__main__':
  2160→    main()
